docs.title: cfd-viz
docs.text: Lightweight visualization CLI for structured CFD datasets.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install cfd-viz

# display a mesh
cfd-viz mesh grid.su2

# display a contour
cfd-viz contour solution.h5 --field pres

See the Installation page for more options, or the User Guide to get started.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • Python 3.11 or later

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text: pip install cfd-viz

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/uahypersonics/cfd-viz.git
cd cfd-viz
pip install -e .

docs.title: Development Installation
docs.text:
pip install -e ".[dev]"

This installs testing and linting tools (pytest, pytest-cov, ruff).

docs.text:

docs.title: plot_contour(x, y, field, *, field_name='field', levels=50, figsize=(10, 6), title=None, filename=None, show=True)
docs.text:
Plot a filled contour of a scalar field on a structured grid.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default x ndarray

x-coordinates, shape (ni, nj) or (ni, nj, 1).

required y ndarray

y-coordinates, same shape as x.

required field ndarray

Scalar field data, same shape as x.

required field_name str

Label for the colorbar.

'field' levels int

Number of contour levels.

50 figsize tuple[float, float]

Figure size (width, height) in inches.

(10, 6) title str | None

Plot title. Defaults to the field name.

None filename str | None

If given, save the figure to this path.

None show bool

Whether to call plt.show().

True

Returns:

Type Description Figure

The matplotlib Figure.

docs.title: plot_mesh(x, y, *, skip=1, figsize=(10, 6), title='Mesh', filename=None, show=True)
docs.text:
Plot a 2-D structured mesh as grid lines.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default x ndarray

x-coordinates, shape (ni, nj) or (ni, nj, 1).

required y ndarray

y-coordinates, same shape as x.

required skip int

Plot every skip-th grid line in each direction. Useful for dense meshes where plotting every line is slow.

1 figsize tuple[float, float]

Figure size (width, height) in inches.

(10, 6) title str

Plot title.

'Mesh' filename str | None

If given, save the figure to this path.

None show bool

Whether to call plt.show().

True

Returns:

Type Description Figure

The matplotlib Figure.

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text:
cfd-viz provides two interfaces for visualizing structured CFD data:

  1. CLI — quick inspection from the terminal
  2. Python API — scriptable plotting for notebooks and automation

Both interfaces read data through cfd-io, so any format cfd-io supports (HDF5, Plot3D, Tecplot, SU2, split binary) works out of the box.

  • CLI Usage
  • Python API

docs.title: Python API
docs.text: The plotting functions can be used directly in scripts and notebooks.

docs.title: Mesh
docs.text:
from cfd_io import read_file
from cfd_viz.mesh import plot_mesh

ds = read_file("grid.su2")
plot_mesh(ds.grid.x, ds.grid.y, skip=2)

Save to file without showing:

plot_mesh(ds.grid.x, ds.grid.y, skip=2, filename="mesh.png", show=False)

docs.title: Contour
docs.text:
from cfd_io import read_file
from cfd_viz.contour import plot_contour

ds = read_file("solution.h5")
plot_contour(ds.grid.x, ds.grid.y, ds.flow["pres"].data, field_name="pres")

Save to file without showing:

plot_contour(
    ds.grid.x, ds.grid.y, ds.flow["pres"].data,
    field_name="pres",
    levels=100,
    filename="pres.png",
    show=False,
)

docs.title: Working with NumPy Arrays Directly
docs.text:
The plotting functions accept plain NumPy arrays — no cfd-io dependency required:

import numpy as np
from cfd_viz.mesh import plot_mesh

x, y = np.meshgrid(np.linspace(0, 1, 50), np.linspace(0, 0.5, 30), indexing="ij")
plot_mesh(x, y)

docs.title: Mesh
docs.text:
Display a structured mesh:

cfd-viz mesh grid.su2

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --skip N -s Plot every N-th grid line (default: 1) --output FILE -o Save figure to file instead of showing --title TEXT -t Plot title (default: "Mesh") --grid-in FILE -g Separate grid file (for split formats)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# skip every 4th line for a dense mesh
cfd-viz mesh grid.su2 --skip 4

# save to file
cfd-viz mesh grid.su2 --skip 2 --output mesh.png

# split format
cfd-viz mesh grid.s8 --grid-in grid.s8

docs.title: Contour
docs.text:
Display a filled contour of a scalar field:

cfd-viz contour solution.h5 --field pres

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --field NAME -f Flow variable to plot (required) --output FILE -o Save figure to file instead of showing --title TEXT -t Plot title (default: field name) --levels N -l Number of contour levels (default: 50) --grid-in FILE -g Separate grid file (for split formats)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# plot pressure contours
cfd-viz contour solution.h5 --field pres

# save with custom levels
cfd-viz contour solution.h5 --field temp --levels 100 --output temp.png

# split format with separate grid file
cfd-viz contour flow.s8 --grid-in grid.s8 --field pres

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --version -V Show version and exit --debug Enable debug logging --help Show help and exit
